heading: Video voyeurism
label: 1801 Video voyeurism
notes: Short Title of 2004 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–495, § 1118 Stat. 3999Dec. 23, 2004“This Act [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Video Voyeurism Prevention Act of 2004’.”
amendments: Short Title of 2004 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–495, § 1118 Stat. 3999Dec. 23, 2004“This Act [enacting this chapter] may be cited as the ‘Video Voyeurism Prevention Act of 2004’.”

children.label: a
children.text: Whoever, in the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States, has the intent to capture an image of a private area of an individual without their consent, and knowingly does so under circumstances in which the individual has a reasonable expectation of privacy, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.
the term “capture”, with respect to an image, means to videotape, photograph, film, record by any means, or broadcast;
the term “broadcast” means to electronically transmit a visual image with the intent that it be viewed by a person or persons;
the term “a private area of the individual” means the naked or undergarment clad genitals, pubic area, buttocks, or female breast of that individual;
the term “female breast” means any portion of the female breast below the top of the areola; and

children.label: A
children.text: circumstances in which a reasonable person would believe that he or she could disrobe in privacy, without being concerned that an image of a private area of the individual was being captured; or

children.label: B
children.text: circumstances in which a reasonable person would believe that a private area of the individual would not be visible to the public, regardless of whether that person is in a public or private place.

children.label: c
children.text: This section does not prohibit any lawful law enforcement, correctional, or intelligence activity.
